title: Replace app-server builder with codex exec adapter
summary: Replace the 941-line codex_harness_builder_adapter.py (which manually implements JSON-RPC over app-server stdio) with a ~150-line CodexExecBuilderAdapter that uses 'codex exec --experimental-json'. This aligns with how Cyrus and cc-connect integrate Codex.
builder_prompt:
## Goal
Replace the Codex app-server JSON-RPC builder with a simpler codex exec builder.

## Steps
1. Create src/spec_orch/services/codex_exec_builder_adapter.py:
   - Class CodexExecBuilderAdapter implementing BuilderAdapter Protocol
   - ADAPTER_NAME = 'codex_exec', AGENT_NAME = 'codex'
   - run() method spawns: codex exec --experimental-json --sandbox workspace-write --skip-git-repo-check <prompt>
   - Read JSONL events from stdout line by line
   - Write events to telemetry/incoming_events.jsonl
   - Track final message from item.completed events where item.type == 'agent_message'
   - Track plan from turn.plan.updated events
   - Detect turn.completed and turn.failed events
   - Run compliance check from domain.compliance on the events
   - Handle process timeout (absolute_timeout_seconds)
   - Write builder_report.json in same format as existing adapter
   - If builder_prompt is None, return skipped result
2. Move codex_harness_builder_adapter.py to src/spec_orch/services/_archived/
3. Update RunController (or cli.py injection) to use CodexExecBuilderAdapter as default
4. Update tests:
   - Create new fake codex exec scripts that output JSONL to stdout
   - Update test_codex_harness_builder_adapter.py -> test_codex_exec_builder_adapter.py
   - Update integration tests
   - Keep compliance tests working (they should use domain.compliance directly)
5. Delete pi_builder_adapter.py (dead code, single-line alias)
6. Run full test suite

## codex exec JSONL event format reference
Each line is a JSON object with a 'type' field:
- {"type": "item.started", "item": {"id": "...", "type": "command_execution"}}
- {"type": "item.completed", "item": {"id": "...", "type": "agent_message", "text": "..."}}
- {"type": "item.completed", "item": {"id": "...", "type": "command_execution", "exit_code": 0}}
- {"type": "turn.completed", "usage": {...}}
- {"type": "turn.failed", "error": "..."}

## Verification
python -m pytest -q && python -m ruff check src/ && python -m mypy src/

context.architecture_notes: After SPC-P0-3, RunController accepts BuilderAdapter via constructor injection. domain/compliance.py has the compliance functions. The new adapter must satisfy the BuilderAdapter Protocol from domain/protocols.py.
constraints: Compliance detection must still work on the new event format
All tests must pass after migration

acceptance_criteria: codex_exec_builder_adapter.py exists and is under 200 lines
Compliance detection works on codex exec JSONL events
